Fiddle with subgraph shapes once https://github.com/mermaid-js/mermaid/issues/2752 is done Categories: History

title: My Relationship With Society
content:
My Relationship With Society # Upbringing # When I was a child, my life was very contrived. I don’t remember being given any responsibilities by my parents that were strongly important and self-“owned”. I helped out my parents for sure, but only by following very direct commands. I didn’t have many experiences along the lines of “accomplish this important task (with real consequences)” that didn’t involve micromanagement.
My schooling was also generally like this, as well as my other activities.
Beyond some resistance to chores I didn’t see the point in doing, I was lucky enough to manage this lifestyle fairly well. I was not a very rebellious child, and succeeded in school (academically, not socially). I directed much of my free energy and time to exploring other worlds in books and video games.
College # College living was amazing to me. When I moved into my first dorm, I reveled in the freedom living on my own afforded me. I could do whatever I wanted whenever, without anyone interrupting or micromanaging me.
At the same time, I felt like it was easier than ever for me to make social connections with others; I fondly remember being a part of the community of people that formed on the same floor as me freshman year.
This new life experience persisted throughout college, perhaps waning a bit in excitement as I got used to it, became more busy with my classes, and moved into a smaller apartment with two friends.
Post Graduation # Right before graduation, I remember realizing that I was about to jump headfirst into the real world, and had not done much thinking at all about what that even meant, let alone what I should do next.
I liked some research I was doing in my department (Chemical Engineering), and was less stoked about the types of jobs people with my degree got immediately post grad (working in large chemical plants). So I applied to some PhD programs, and even got accepted to one.
During this time, I saw an email on a mailing list from a small startup (Arzeda) that was looking for interns and/or employees. I started interning for them and liked the work, which was mostly data analysis and programming like my research. They offered me a full time job and I subsequently dropped my PhD offer and started working for them.
Looking back, I feel that initial email saved me from a much worse alternate future. My sense is that going forward with a PhD would have just been a delaying of entering the “real world”; I would have simply perpetuated my current familiar academic environment. I think this would have led to a similar, but probably harsher entry to the real world later, which may not have gone as smoothly.
Early Work # Now that I was making money for the first time in my life ($80k/yr, much more than I could spend), I realized I didn’t know what to do with it.
I discovered Mister Money Mustache and other FIRE bloggers, who gave me a clear answer: save the money, let it grow, and use it to buy free time later. I accepted this strategy wholeheartedly (I was probably well predisposed to it).
At this time, I was living with my new girlfriend Claire and a couple friends from college. Life was good, but the larger community from my freshman year of college was not present anymore. I’m a pretty introverted person and, left to my own devices, will naturally spend most of my time in solitary pursuits like books/games when others aren’t around. I was not dissatisfied with this current lifestyle, but likely missed out on some friendships and opportunities I would have had with a larger social circle.
Career Progression # As I continued working at Arzeda, I started feeling less productive and less able to grow than I did initially. My work was very self directed and I wasn’t seeing a lot of success with it, and I didn’t feel like I was getting good help from my coworkers. I had solidified into mostly doing pure software development, which is something my PhD scientist coworkers were not well trained in.
This, coupled with my realization that software developers can make a lot more than $80k/yr in Seattle led me to start job hunting.
I send out some applications with low expectations, but got a lot of interest right away. I was lucky enough to actually be quite good at the programming challenges that software interviews consisted of, and was accepted at all the companies that initially responded to me. This included Palantir and Google, both of which offered me staggeringly large salaries (in the ballpark of $250k).
I ended up taking Google’s offer, fearing that the work environment at Palantir would be too intense for someone like me with no formal software dev training (I was pretty much 100% self taught).
Now # With my job at Google netting me enough money to retire at the current point in time, I feel like I should start answering the questions about what I should do with myself I have decided to put off for most of my professional career. There seem to be a couple options:
Keep working as long as I can, then take all my money and give it to people who can enact the most positive change in the world. Retire now, and dedicate myself to this positive change. Retire now, and live for myself and the people close to me. My current “plan” is some hybrid approach - keep cruising at Google as long as I can for money, and try to do positive stuff in my free time, with a healthy dose of entertaining myself and friends.
Claire and I are married with a house, but don’t plan to have kids. We live pretty independently, and I wouldn’t say we are “traditionally married” - we both work, don’t share finances, and don’t have asymmetric responsibilities to each other (like breadwinner/homemaker). Right now we just provide each other with companionship/entertainment, which has been great :).
I’m pretty satisfied with my current life. I am a bit concerned that my lack of connection to a larger community of people that I life with/near will not be great long term, especially as I get older and less self-sufficient/energetic. It takes me a lot of energy even now to get out and socialize - I feel like I could easily be trapped in a depressing lack of community later of life when I have even less energy.

title: Timeline
content:
A Timeline of My Life # https://jubishop.com/about-me.html is some inspiration for how to make this look prettier.
See https://maps.google.com/timeline for location data. Also my Google calendar for activity data.
August 17th, 1985 Parents Married
Sept 14th, 1993 Born at swedish hospital in seattle (TODO add address)
1998 Preschool at North Seattle Fives. I remember doing an exercise where we discussed if we preferred sleeping on our side, back, or stomach. In it, I chose stomach because I liked the light colored female cartoon (as opposed to the blue male back sleeper or the young child side sleeper). Maybe I also already slept on my stomach, but I have been a stomach sleeper all my life.
Did a lot of boy scouting stuff. Went to Camp Parsons many times.
Snow Camping with scouts by Stevens pass
Camp Fire Camp at Carkeek Park across many summers (I was “chipmunk”)
2003 Trip to Chicago
Random 2003 Journalling
2004 Journalling, creative writing
Summer 2005 Took trip to California, also Camp Parsons and other stuff
2006 Moved to Purdue house from greenlake
Feb 2006 Took trip to Lithuania (see journals)
A Middle School Spring Break I spent the whole week immersed in Dungeon Siege with Dad at work and Mom/Vaiva away on a trip. I felt so free from pressure to do what other people wanted from me, and it was exhilarating. I distinctly remember making a cream corn chowder soup recipe all by myself, of my own volition, and being really proud of how it turned out.
Karate stuff
July 21st - August 8th 2007 Sailboat Trip with Frankes
Summer 2008 Road trip to California
May 8th, 2010 Summited Mt. St. Helens and skied down.
Summer 2011 Took Trip to India (see journal)
Sept 2012 Moved into Poplar Hall with Andrew Tat on the Honors floor.
2012 Cozy Cottage
Snow camping with Honors ppl by Snoqualmie Pass
March 15th, 2013 Parents finalized divorce
Summer 2013 Went on Trip to Rome (pictures). Had fling with Tessa Pontow (first ever real romance). Coursework. Took trip across Europe afterward.
Winter 2014 Talked to Claire for the first time when each of us were working on lab reports in the Organic Chemistry computer lab (that had the right software for reading IR or NMR).
Summer 2014 Made shitty Urban Archives website. First professional software development gig.
Sept 2014 Moved into Wilsonian with Bryant and Danny.
Winter 2014? Did Husky Winter Sports
Dec 15, 2015 Got email from Hyak supercomputer mailing list about position at Arzeda. Subsequently interviewed there and was so excited about the interview that I started working there before my graduation one day a week and decided not to enter a PhD program.
Summer 2015 Had sex for the first time with Maya Monroe, who I met at a party + was somehow partially involved with Husky Winter Sports
2015-2016 Met David Lin, Trent, Mac, and co. Learned Magic the Gathering and played some cubes with them.
2015? Would climb up this fire escape behind Wilsonian apartments to make out with Claire.
June 2016 Graduated from UW (transcript)
Sept 2016 Moved into 4722 12th Ave NE Apt. 502 with Claire, Bryant, and David Zeng. Played a lot of Overwatch 1.
October 2016 Dad retired
June 2017 Some research I did in the Pfaendtner lab was finally published!
Sept 2017 Moved into Laurelhurst Apartments with Claire and Will.
Oct 2017 Got April and May.
Sept 2018 Swapped out Will for Ryan.
2020-2021 Won Xbox in vaccine lottery and then sold it.
Jan 25, 2021 Reached out to Conor MacEvilly and started house search.
August 2021 Moved into 5501 Brooklyn Ave NE. Got first tenants (Payton and Linh). I was impressed that Payton was able to sell some generative NFT art for thousands of dollars.
October 2021 Got Oculus Quest 2 and started paying a bunch of Beat Saber
Dec 22, 2021 Got married in new house living room with christmas decorations. Had lots of OK lemon cake.
Jan 2022 Started working with Michael Lustgarten on https://github.com/kovasap/biomarker-correlator.
01/16/2022 Week in review Started on the biomarker correlator project with Michael Lustgarten, made good progress. Clojure is still kinda frustrating to work with, so many functions to learn! It seems like there is a lot of initial breath to master before getting really in the flow.
Modded Skyrim and started a playthrough. It’s fun to get distracted by all the random things in the world :).
Took lunch home every day at work, which made me feel a lot more productive. My “after-lunch-slump” was almost totally gone. Although I did tend to overeat at night. I think this is a solvable problem though.
Made progress on the Chamber analysis report, coming really close to totally cleaning up the A/B user journey. Mukul is leaving the team, mixed feelings. Installed tenant dishwasher with new water detecting valve. A bit concerned that my daisy chain of adapters might fail in some obscure way, but my testing didn’t catch any issues.
Had a positive Chamber calibration server debugging session this week, where I was able to identify why machine counts for a specific architecture were dropping to zero. This involved incremental progress with various dremel queries. It was very satisfying to never hit a wall during the process :). Started playing wordle.
Summer 2022 Worked on custom Guitar workflows for ITS and related ITS Bazel code at Google. Got sucked into doing a big refactor of the load_test macro and tidying up other related loose ends.
Summer 2022 Did camera lifelogging project. See pictures at https://narrativeapp.com/timeline/?month=5&year=2022, or downloaded onto my Seagate external drive.
Fall/Winter 2022 Worked on building out a regression detection strategy framework at Google.
Aug 2022 Started working with Ben Blue on WormBot related stuff (https://github.com/kovasap/microscope)
Nov 2022 Started playing Scythe + others with Jakob’s old frat brothers.
Dec 2022 Started working on a board game concept in CLJS (https://github.com/kovasap/cljs-board-game).
Jan 2022 Adam laid off from google.
Jan 2022 Started setting a lot of boulder problems at the IMA crags
Feb 2022 Dark and Darker playtest for Steam NextFest. Super fun!
March 2022 Lots of terra mystica with Jakob’s crew, and R6: Siege with Ben Blue and company
April 2022
The last spell Jedi fallen order Elbow pain from climbing Everything everywhere all at once Dark and darker play test 5, torrent style Russian revolutions podcast Lots of discussion about how to get aligned at work on the Low Config Regression Detection project. Frustrating. Three Body Problem Audiobook Weekend of 4/22/2023 Cubing at Trent’s place (cool throne shower), played mono-red, did OK. Fun with The Last Spell Went to Janette’s, ate lots of homemade sushi and some MREs someone brought, played ROOT, won as the vagabond. Also played Cat Lady, which was fun! Slept not great (no shower, lots of snack food) Started playing Strange Horticulture with Claire, she was super into it. May 2023
Subnautica: Below Zero Ozymandias R6: Siege Some climbing at Amazonia and Index Started nightly journalling June 2023 Got into voidspire tactics and was inspired to try (again) at making my own tactics rpg. Got a lot of good momentum going and am making more progress than i ever have before!
July 2023
Starting to implement the first version of low config regression detection after a very long design process. Started life choices game idea after getting inspired out of the blue on a random run. Met Ben Blue’s friend group in person Got really into Enderal potionomics with claire, many sessions! Played a bunch of marvel snap August 2023
Met AnneFlank on reddit, played some great siege with his crew Called 911 on passed out person on sidewalk with Claire working activitywatch+autojournal on gas Dark and Darker Early Access release, played a bunch with people i ran into again on the discord from the playtests. 8/26: claire in vegas for shay’s birthday. tried on high roller goblin caves, got google sheets -> lifesim data import working, played some terra mystica at zack’s new place (won by a lot with giants by spamming shovels at the end (2 pts. per)) 8/27: played some tm online with 2 others, one using a fan faction. lost playing alchemists despite building almost all buildings and having almost 4 towns. hosted kamigawa draft #2, tied for 1st in two really intense games with cody (me red blue control, him green black midrange) September 2023
9/2: Great climbing day at amazonia. Led Primus and routes to the left with cool roofs. Watched the good place dark and darker, grappling with what i want out of a competitive game (performance expectations of myself) outer wilds echoes of the eye 9/14, 30th birthday. woke up, ate cabbage and carrot stir fry with microwaved potatos and eggs. played enderal and got to just before the final quest (did final companion quests and went to starling city). did laundry. then played dark and darker and killed the cave troll as a cleric for the first time, with a bit of damage from a warlock attempting it. then (3pm) went climbing to little si with chris and stephen. almost did lay of the land clean, figured out top section beta (go right). did godflesh. came home, ate food, had carrot mug cake. finished the good place. 9/16: had big birthday party with ppl from a couple friend groups. played codenames, two rooms and a boom, captain sonar, and azul. super awesome :) 9/17: went to exit 38 with jakob to climb the ascensionist, but it was taken. climbed a bunch of cool stuff on the deception wall instead! then did “dark chambers of our minds” in enderal. Week of 9/18: Played a lot of dark and darker after work on Mon, Tues. Had high expectations for myself as a solo wizard and died a lot. Felt very stressed / jittery. Also had a lot of snack food from the party and snacked a lot in response to the stress (I think). On Wednesday, I instead just watched a Jaygriffyuh stream for ~4 hrs. Still snacked but felt a lot more relaxed. He died a lot too : ). 9/21: Went to world wall with Stephen, Chris, and Jakob. Followed Stephen on Rainy Day Woman and Busta Move (both 12a) and climbed both with minimal hanging! Rogue heavily nerfed in Dark and Darker - happy to die from them less as wizard : ). 9/23: Hung out with claires friends at pike place, then did heist escape room (ok), then scotch egg at pub (good), then mr. b’s meadery (cool!), then pie i made at home (oreo crust, raspberry reduction, vanilla mouse) (good!). hanging out with people is kinda boring sometimes :/. 9/24: Claire bday. finished echoes of the eye, tried 7 wonders duel, had dinner with Claire’s parents. 9/29 - 10/1: Went to squamish with chris, jakob, and holly! 9/29: explored the smoke bluffs, climbing High Boltage Line (hard slab, couldn’t do many moves) and adjacent. Tried to find split asunder and couldn’t, but randomly stumbled upon cabin boy’s office from above and did some routes to the left (not on mtn proj page). Finished up on Laughing Crack, which everyone else tried to jam and i just laybacked, which led to a much faster ascent. Consistently impressed with the level of development here - lots of great signs/bridges/stairs on the trail, and even some rebar ladders drilled into the rock! 9/30: Went to Chek to do frontside 180, an extremely densely bolted 10 pitch climb. 6 pitches up we randomly discovered rock of ages and did emotional rescue, go your own way, and some others. really cool water shaped rock, with scoopy jugs. finished multipitch and saw world wall-esque “The monastery” on the way down. Ended day climbing at electric avenue with 50+ other people. Credulous Hedgehog was pretty fun. At end of day taught Jakob and Chris Pax Pamir and felt like i understood it a lot better. Also played a round of hanabi. 10/1: Climbed the very busy Klahonie Crack (3x in a row on TR, again not jamming and having it be way easier than all the trad leaders) as well as Cardhu Crack. Cardhu was very long and we ran out of rope, leading to us needing to build a trad anchor towards the bottom to belay. October 2023
10/2: Productive day at work fixing user issues. Bought a orange and black bike on fb marketplace and biked with it back home, got very wet. 10/7-8: Got into battle brothers again after trying wartales and the iron oath. Fixed bikes, extended handlebars on blue bike. Hosted board games with Jakob crew, with Stephanie (her birthday near!), ate lots of snacks/cake, slept poorly. 10/13: Sent Lay of the Land!!! First 5.12a outside send. Worked on it over ~4 sessions and went throught the whole progression of impossible -> possible -> doable -> done. 10/14: Went to sad UW post office to pick up high refresh rate 1080p monitor. 1080p is hard to go back to after 1440p :(. I did notice the refresh rate though. Going to order a 1440p 144hz and see if my graphics card (gtx 1060) can handle it. Seems like I can run Siege at 80fps at least. See notes from all my monitor exploration at https://github.com/kovasap/website/blob/master/content/docs/gaming/monitor-notes.md. 10/15-18: Played more Battle Brothers, got farther than before and had more fun! 10/19: Met with Cheyenne virtually about gamedev stuff after listening to Darkest Dungeon creator interview. Talked him through my game ideas and got some inspiration. I think I want to go further on my board game idea. 10/19: Finally finished Enderal and read “The Butcher of Ark”. Kinda underwhelmed with the ending, but happy I finished it. 10/20: Played a bunch of CS2 Wingman with Cheyenne - super fun! 10/21: Started Dark Souls 1. Is good. Had a board game night and played mysterium and co-op trick taking game. 10/24: Beat Qualaat in DS1 with really crappy weapons. Accidentally beat ceasless discharge afterward by knocking it off a ledge. 10/25: Went to Optispan and Ora with Ben! Met people and started work log. 10/26: Long discussion at work about how to make my change to Perfgate storage (adding Autobelay database client) GCE compatible. Pretty epic process for something that would be so simple in Python/Clojure. Played physical Wingspan copy with Stephanie and Tristan at Arthur’s. 10/27: Played some Siege Halloween mode with Danny/Hari. Fun and nostalgic :). 10/28: Route setting at the IMA! Set two good problems in B4, I brown V4 using the natural crack and a purple V2. Got to Anor Londo in Dark Souls. 10/29: Went to Gameworks for Tristans Birthday. Was OK. Met Leela who’s dad died recently, was a lot. 10/31: Did some lights-out climbing at the IMA for halloween. Chris had a great hobbit costume with a sweet cloak. November 2023
11/4: Addison broke her leg on the stairs. Drove to hospital and back, did planning for handrail install. 11/5: More routesetting; made a white problem that started and ended at ground level, and it reversible! 11/9: Met with Taylor at optispan and started data platform design document. 11/10: Installed handrail, which went very well. Then the downstairs toilet clogged and flooded! Spent a while drying up floors, then paid $1k for a plumber to come out. I should have done more price checking, but I was stressed :(. 11/11: Sewer scope done, no problems shown, except some root intrusion possibility on way out to street. 11/9 - 11/11: Started playing Dark and Darker with Hari. Game is hard with mostly warlock, but I’m improving I think? 11/12: Went to Uwajimaya with Claire, made sushi. Progressed in catacombs (dark souls), played some CS with Cheyenne and Ben Blue. Tried Monster Sanctuary and got Pathfinder: WOTR. 11/17: Got a fitbit. Pretty dissapointed with the data collection/extraction :(. 11/18: Slept poorly this week, pretty low energy day recovering. 11/19: Had some ideas for tactics game, but overwhelmed trying to implement them. Good advice at https://www.reddit.com/r/gamedev/s/YAzQtt5gOq. Started getting back into Oxygen Not Included - super fun! 11/20: Debugging EDivisive YAQS, hard. 11/21: Debugging ANOTHER EDivisive YAQS, hard. Good climbing session. Found amazing data file at work with strange funny phrases. 11/22: Met random guy (Josh?) in bike room at Google, had breakfast. Made some good progress on accelerator tests. Got Chevy Volt car rental. Made good progress on Arbor tree ONI build. 11/24: Thanksgiving at Claire’s. Fed deer apples. 11/25: Bad sleep. Played a lot of Dark and Darker warlock. New progression systems with white gear lobbies are great! Had Ezells at Janette’s and player Everdell, super fun! Did well with a blue card clocktower strategy. At this point I tried moving this lifelogging process to mementodb. The data from that can be found at https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1nZ-iliU7MtRPoAdP-HbjBees6u3-NIlicHRfewD0Q7A/edit?gid=1108618816#gid=1108618816. This was easy to use, but led to a lot of logging of micro-events. I think going forward I want to try more macro-level logging.
July 2024
Went to Chris’s wedding, borrowed Phil’s tenant’s car to do so. Backpacked through the enchantments with Jakob and company. See pictures here. August 2024
